_note: Auto-generated at session end. Do not edit manually. Before regenerating, the AI must archive the previous version of this file to .context/sessions/<generated_at-ISO>.json so machine-readable history is preserved. Commit with CHANGELOG.md, PROJECT_STATE.md, and SESSION_LOG.md.
session_summary: Built the OA Judge end-to-end (engine + Flask API + HackerRank-style UI), added syntax highlighting, a curated edge-case test layer, a Desktop launcher, and initialized the context logs. Follow-up: fixed the editor's paste-misalignment and added Enter auto-indent.
current_phase: Phase 3 — Working product; expanding the problem set and polishing UX
build_state: 11 of 13 problems runnable (Flipkart x3, DE Shaw x3, Millennium x2, Uber x3), each with sample + curated-edge + random hidden tests, all reference-verified. Full gate green: every reference ACs its suite (17-20 tests each); every stub is rejected. UI has syntax highlighting, LC/OA modes, Find Failing Test (stress), history, and a one-click Desktop launcher. cisco-q1 and cisco-q2 are statement-only (no verified reference yet).
files_modified_this_session: problems/* (14 problem packages: problem.json, statement.md, editorial.md, stub.*, reference.*, generator.py, tests/{sample,edge,hidden})
commands_run: python3 verify_all.py            (integration gate — all runnable problems PASS)
python3 smoke_test.py            (every reference ACs its suite; every stub rejected)
python3 make_hidden.py <id>      (regenerate hidden tests from references, all 11)
g++ -std=c++17 -O2 …             (compile + verify references against brute force)
powershell.exe Invoke-WebRequest http://127.0.0.1:5000 (verify WSL2 localhost forwarding)
agy-delegate.sh --tier pro --yolo (6 parallel Gemini subagents: frontend, packaging, research)

decisions_made.decision: References are the source of truth; hidden test outputs are generated by running them, never hand-written
decisions_made.rationale: Hand-derived expected values repeatedly turned out wrong; generation from a brute-force-verified reference is trustworthy

decisions_made.decision: Ambiguous OA statements commit to a single reading; alternatives go in the editorial
decisions_made.rationale: A judge must have one correct answer per test; cannot present two readings to the solver

decisions_made.decision: Hand-written syntax highlighter over a transparent textarea instead of CodeMirror/CDN
decisions_made.rationale: Keeps the app fully offline and dependency-free; overlay verified lossless so it stays caret-aligned

decisions_made.decision: Generators take an integer size hint (argv[2]), small default
decisions_made.rationale: Categorical size strings produced megabyte inputs when the tooling passed integers

decisions_made.decision: Curated edge cases (tests/edge/) required per problem in addition to random cases
decisions_made.rationale: Random-only coverage misses bounds/degenerate/adversarial cases that real OAs ship

errors_encountered.error: stress.py recompiled the reference every iteration (~1000 compiles → request timeout)
errors_encountered.resolution: Compile reference (and user) once and reuse; skip shrink when the first counterexample is already small

errors_encountered.error: Uber generators used string size-categories; integer sizes hit the 'large' branch → megabyte inputs
errors_encountered.resolution: Rewrote all three to an integer size hint; regenerated hidden tests

errors_encountered.error: Nested wsl.exe from inside WSL failed to start the launcher server during testing
errors_encountered.resolution: Identified as a test-environment artifact (real Explorer double-click is a clean entry); verified each component separately

errors_encountered.error: User saw 'failed to fetch' on Find Failing Test
errors_encountered.resolution: The server window had been closed; not a code bug

errors_encountered.error: Could not machine-verify LeetCode/GfG links (HTTP 403 to curl and WebFetch)
errors_encountered.resolution: Curated links by hand-confidence and dropped uncertain ones; links remain unverified

errors_encountered.error: Syntax-highlight overlay drifted out of line-alignment after pasting a block (edits landed a line below the caret)
errors_encountered.resolution: Root cause was fractional font-size (13.5px) + ratio line-height (1.55) rounding differently per line and accumulating. Switched textarea, highlight <pre>, and line-numbers to fixed 13px/20px integer metrics; added paste normalization (CRLF->LF, tabs->spaces) and Enter auto-indent. Verified with overlay screenshots (25-line block and the exact millennium-q1 stub) showing zero drift.

errors_encountered.error: User reported the editor STILL misaligned after the CSS fix
errors_encountered.resolution: Not a code bug — the browser was serving cached old app.js/style.css. Proven: served assets contain the fix and the overlay screenshot aligns. Added @app.after_request no-cache headers in app/server.py so the local tool never serves stale editor assets. User must hard-refresh once (Ctrl+Shift+R) or relaunch to drop the already-cached copy.

next_action: Make cisco-q1-drone-delivery and cisco-q2-sniper-detector runnable. Specs are in _migrated_raw/cisco/coding.md: q1 = min-moves 0-1 BFS/Dijkstra over state (row,col,battery,vouchersLeft), samples 7/11/-1; q2 = sliding-window sniper detection, output 'flag sniper' per line. For each: write reference.cpp, cross-check vs an independent brute force on random inputs, add stub.cpp (reproduce the provided struct InputData + parse_input harness), generator.py (integer size hint), sample tests from the examples, curated tests/edge/*.in; set problem.json runnable=true and languages=["cpp"]; then make_hidden.py, verify_all.py, smoke_test.py.
